real_architecture_summary: Proposal/spec objects feed search and gating in project/research/search/evaluator.py and project/research/gating.py, which then feed candidate_discovery_scoring, eval robustness/cost models, bridge evaluation, and blueprint compilation. Cost semantics are split across project/core/execution_costs.py, project/eval/cost_model.py, and research-side helpers.
top_invariants: PIT joins must never leak bars after the event timestamp.
Execution-cost units must be consistent across core, eval, search, and bridge paths.
Funding carry must not be repackaged as negative transaction cost in robustness stress panels.

findings.title: Execution cost units diverge across core, eval, and research paths
findings.evidence: project/core/execution_costs.py resolves a one-sided fee+slippage total, project/research/search/evaluator.py subtracts that once, while project/eval/cost_model.py applies 2 * (fee + slippage) as round-trip cost.
findings.why_it_matters: The same fee config produces different after-cost returns depending on the path that computed them.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Compare resolve_execution_costs(), estimate_transaction_cost_bps(), apply_cost_model(), and evaluate_hypothesis_batch() under the same fee/slippage config.
findings.suggested_remediation: Adopt one repo-wide cost unit contract and rename fields/sites so per-side and round-trip values cannot be confused.

findings.title: Funding carry can make stressed-cost robustness improve as costs are stressed
findings.evidence: build_event_return_frame() records cost_return = per_trade_cost - funding_carry_return, which can go negative, and evaluate_structural_robustness() subtracts that negative cost again, making higher stress multipliers improve pnl retention.
findings.why_it_matters: A cost-stress panel can certify fragile candidates as more robust when stressed harder.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Generate a short-side return frame with positive funding_rate_scaled and feed the negative costs_bps into evaluate_structural_robustness().
findings.suggested_remediation: Stress transaction costs separately from funding carry and clamp transaction-cost inputs to non-negative values.

findings.title: Funding carry is applied as a flat trade adjustment independent of holding horizon
findings.evidence: _funding_carry_return() reads one scalar funding rate and applies it once per trade regardless of horizon length or funding-interval count.
findings.why_it_matters: A 5-minute hold and a 1-hour hold can receive identical funding carry, distorting horizon comparisons.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Call build_event_return_frame() with the same event and constant funding rate but different horizons and compare funding_carry_return.
findings.suggested_remediation: Accrue carry over the actual holding window using aligned funding timestamps and interval counts.

findings.title: Research helper integrate_execution_costs is broken and does not call the shared core utility correctly
findings.evidence: integrate_execution_costs() calls resolve_execution_costs(symbol) positionally, but resolve_execution_costs is keyword-only and requires a full cost-config contract.
findings.why_it_matters: A purported shared research integration path fails immediately and cannot share semantics with the maintained core path.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Import integrate_execution_costs() and call it on a dummy DataFrame and symbol.
findings.suggested_remediation: Delete the dead helper or rewrite it against the actual resolve_execution_costs() keyword contract.

findings.title: The repo marks after-cost outputs as including funding carry even when actual carry coverage can be zero
findings.evidence: candidate_discovery_service hard-codes after_cost_includes_funding_carry=True, while candidate_discovery_scoring separately computes funding_carry_eval_coverage and gating only includes carry when funding fields are present.
findings.why_it_matters: Consumers can assume net expectancy already includes carry economics when the observed coverage is actually zero.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Trace the hard-coded flag in candidate_discovery_service.py and compare it with funding_carry_eval_coverage on candidate sets lacking funding columns.
findings.suggested_remediation: Derive the flag from observed coverage or split formula capability from observed carry coverage.

test_coverage_assessment: Temporal/PIT tests are stronger than economic-consistency tests. Current coverage does not catch cross-path cost divergence, negative-cost stress improvement, horizon-insensitive funding accrual, or the broken research-side cost helper.
docs_specs_code_contradictions: project/features/context_states.py::TEMPORAL_CONTRACT says uses_current_observation=False, but calculate_ms_trend_probabilities() uses current-bar rolling std by default.
after_cost_includes_funding_carry is documented in diagnostics as always true, while actual carry inclusion depends on available funding fields.
suggested_regression_tests: A single fee-config equivalence test across core/eval/research.
A negative-cost stress test that must never improve under higher cost stress.
open_questions_or_unknowns: What the canonical meaning of cost_bps is meant to be across the repo: per side, per fill, or round trip.
